columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The text on page 2 states: "The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes..." and "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85)... metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." This indicates reporting for the "de novo" (synchronous) group.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The text on page 2 states: "The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes compared with developing metastases after local therapy (recurrent or metachronous disease)." and "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with ... recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." This indicates reporting for the "recurrent" (metachronous) group.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The "PURPOSE" section on page 1 states: "We examined efﬁcacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk." The "METHODS" section on page 2 defines: "High-volume disease was deﬁned as visceral metastases and/or four or more bone metastases with one or more beyond the vertebral column and pelvis." The "RESULTS" section on page 1 reports: "Dar-olutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume (hazard ratio [HR], 0.69; 95%CI, 0.57 to 0.82)..."
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The "PURPOSE" section on page 1 states: "We examined efﬁcacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk." The "METHODS" section on page 2 states: "Low-volume and low-risk disease were deﬁned as not meeting the" (criteria for high-volume/high-risk). The "RESULTS" section on page 1 reports: "...and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival beneﬁt (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41to 1.13)."
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
